prerequisites: Physical console access to the device (serial or USB console cable)
Ability to power-cycle the device (for ROMMON-based recovery)
Know the device platform and OS to select the correct procedure
Authorization to perform password recovery (security policy may require approval)
procedure_name: Password Recovery and AAA Lockout Recovery (iosxr)
purpose: Recover access to a device when admin credentials are lost or AAA server is unreachable [iosxr — method: Root user password recovery via auxiliary/ROMMON; platforms: Cisco 8000, NCS 5500, NCS 540/560, ASR 9000]

rollback.action: No rollback needed — only the password is changed
Original configuration is preserved throughout the process

steps.action: Connect console cable and reboot the device
steps.notes: Console access is mandatory. On Cisco 8000 / NCS, connect to the RP console port.
Send break signal during boot sequence. On Cisco 8000, press Ctrl+C when prompted. Timing varies by platform.

steps.action: Boot into single-user mode or recovery mode
steps.notes: The '-o' flag boots IOS-XR without loading the configuration, similar to IOS-XE's confreg 0x2142. On some platforms, use 'boot <image> -s' for single-user mode.
Once in recovery mode, the system prompts for a new root-system username and password. Enter new credentials when prompted.

steps.action: Apply and commit the password change
steps.notes: After setting the new password, commit the change. The configuration is preserved — only the password changes.
Device reboots with the original configuration plus the new password.

verification.confirms_if: Root access restored with new credentials

verification.look_for: username root-system or configured admin users

warnings: Password recovery requires PHYSICAL console access — there is no remote recovery method
Some organizations disable password recovery for security (NX-OS: 'system no password-recovery', IOS-XE: 'no service password-recovery')
If password recovery is disabled, the ONLY option is to erase the configuration and start fresh
On IOS-XE: confreg 0x2142 skips startup-config on boot — do NOT forget to set it back to 0x2102
Document new credentials securely after recovery
IOS-XR password recovery preserves the full configuration — much simpler than IOS-XE
On older ASR 9000 (pre-eXR): recovery is done from admin mode, not ROMMON
Cisco 8000 with secure boot (IOS-XR 7.5+): ROMMON may require additional authentication
If TACACS/RADIUS is the only auth method and servers are unreachable, see AAA lockout procedure below
